summary: Priority frame is broadly sound: the live top-of-frame (cfk uplift re-run, 3tl legibility) and the new P1 substrate epics (s7ae coordination, 4smp variants) are correctly elevated, and no tier-4 polish blocks any tier-1 work (the three P4 bugs incl. s8q are intentionally parked, not displacing). The single in_progress bead s7ae.1 is genuinely active (its coordination envelope/CLI code is uncommitted in the working tree, matching its design exactly). Two real hygiene defects: the P1 scale-hardening epic 1xc has confirmed-live severe bugs but zero child beads (work anchored only in a GH checklist), and all four genuine `decision` beads have already-resolved decisions still sitting open (one, f94, is now pure execution work mistyped as a decision).

gaps.proposed_title: Chunk full insight rebuild into bounded per-batch transactions with progress rows
gaps.why: 1xc's OWN design names this as a tier-1 CONFIRMED-LIVE bug: 'full insight rebuild runs as ONE transaction -> 6GB WAL + minutes-long write lock on the live archive'. The epic has zero child beads, so its highest-severity item is not actionable in the tracker (only in gh#2465), violating the beads-is-tracker doctrine.
gaps.design: In the storage/insights rebuild path, replace the single wrapping transaction with bounded per-batch transactions (fixed session/message batch size), emitting progress rows so a crash resumes mid-rebuild. Cap WAL growth; verify against the live 16K-session / 5M-message archive shape.
gaps.acceptance: Insight rebuild on a large-archive copy holds WAL under a bounded cap (e.g. the journal_size_limit) and never holds a minutes-long write lock; progress is observable; a scale-tier test on the synthetic corpus generator would have caught the one-transaction regression.

gaps.proposed_title: Audit global PKs derived from non-unique local coordinates (run_ref collision class siblings)
gaps.why: 1xc's design: 'the run_ref global-PK collision class was fixed (#2464) -- audit for siblings (any global PK derived from non-unique local coordinates)'. No child bead carries this audit.
gaps.design: Enumerate every global primary/unique key constructed from local coordinates (session-local index, per-artifact ordinal, etc.); prove uniqueness at real scale (duplicate native ids, hash collisions, 16K+ sessions); fix any collision-prone key like #2464.
gaps.acceptance: A written audit lists every global-PK-from-local-coordinate site with a uniqueness verdict at scale; each collision-prone site has a fix or a follow-up bead.

gaps.proposed_title: Scale-regression lane: seeded large-archive tier / live-copy probe wired into optional lanes
gaps.why: This is 1xc's explicit epic-terminal AC ('a scale-regression lane exists that would have caught each shipped bug class, wired into the optional lanes'). Nothing currently carries it, so the epic can never reach its own terminal state.
gaps.design: Extend the synthetic corpus generator to a large-archive tier (or a sanitized live-copy probe) and register a devtools optional lane that exercises rebuild/WAL/PK invariants at that scale.
gaps.acceptance: `devtools lab lanes` lists a scale-regression lane; running it reproduces the WAL/lock and PK-collision bug classes on the seeded tier; wired as an optional (not per-PR) lane.

inconsistencies.evidence: Typed `decision` but description reads 'DECIDED (operator, 2026-07-03): KILL' with a concrete Execution block (delete ui/tui, its command/inventory registrations, tests, the Textual dependency, then regenerate topology projection + render all --check). No decision remains open; this is deletion execution work.
inconsistencies.fix: Retype to `task` (P3). The decision is recorded; the remaining scope is a mechanical removal PR with the execution steps already enumerated.

inconsistencies.evidence: Typed `decision`, but description ends 'Status: adopted; apply when prioritizing/designing context-loop, web, and CLI surfaces.' The decision is made, not pending.
inconsistencies.fix: Resolved-decision-still-open: close as a recorded doctrine (or move the doctrine text into docs and close). It should not sit in the open/ready decision queue as if undecided.

inconsistencies.evidence: Typed `decision`; 'Status: adopt as working doctrine; the Sinex-side emitter is future work gated on the substrate write-leg and Sinex production restore.' The decision is adopted; only downstream implementation (a separate feature) remains.
inconsistencies.fix: Resolved-decision-still-open: close the decision; track the Sinex-emitter implementation as a separate feature gated on the rii write-leg rather than keeping the decision bead open.

inconsistencies.evidence: Typed `decision`; 'Status: working doctrine; revisit when beads ingestion lands.' The beads-vs-assertions boundary is decided (working doctrine), not an open question.
inconsistencies.fix: Resolved-decision-still-open: close as recorded doctrine with a note to revisit at beads-ingestion; do not carry an adopted boundary as open decision work.

inconsistencies.evidence: P1 epic scope defers its actual worklist to an external GH checklist: 'Work the checklist on the issue; tier-1 items were observed live' and design says 'gh#2465 checklist is authoritative; work it there'. Per doctrine 'GH issues are external-refs, NOT mirrors', authoritative work should live in beads children, not only in the GH thread.
inconsistencies.fix: Materialize the tier-1 checklist items as child beads (see gaps: rebuild chunking, global-PK sibling audit, scale-regression lane). Keep gh-2465 as an external ref, not the authority.

dep_priority.detail: P1 epic 'Scale-hardening: bugs that only bite on real-scale archives' has 0 child beads yet its AC requires 'every child closed and a scale-regression lane exists', and its design names three concrete tier-1/CONFIRMED-LIVE items (one-transaction insight rebuild -> 6GB WAL + minutes-long lock; run_ref-class global-PK collision sibling audit; scale-regression lane). The epic cannot reach its own terminal state with no children, and the actual work is anchored only in gh#2465. This is the highest-severity correctness epic in the frame yet is unactionable in the tracker.
dep_priority.proposed: Create the three child beads listed under gaps (2 at P1 correctness, 1 at P2 lane) parented to 1xc; treat gh-2465 as an external ref only.

dep_priority.detail: P3 bug 'fts_freshness_state declared twice: reconcile with schema policy' is a schema-policy self-violation risk (DDL appears in tier DDL AND the lifecycle ensure-path; shapes could diverge). Under the frame (correctness > surface hygiene) a live schema-consistency defect on a storage/FTS-readiness column outranks P3 surface work sitting at P2. It is confirmed as a duplication, only the divergence-severity is 're-verify on current source'.
dep_priority.proposed: Raise to P2 (or park explicitly if verified benign). If it survives re-verification as a real divergence risk, it belongs with the storage-correctness tier, not deep P3 backlog. Optionally relate to the 9e5 audit epic (column-honesty / schema-inference audits).

reorg.rationale: Decision is DECIDED (KILL the TUI); remaining scope is a mechanical deletion PR. Retyping from `decision` to `task` makes it a claimable execution item instead of a phantom open decision. (Type change, priority unchanged.)

Flagged and cleared: 'Make deployed Polylogue state trustworthy' reads as a correctness bug that P4 would seem to under-rank, but the description shows it is DELIBERATELY parked ('Parked while prod polylogued.service is deliberately inactive during the devloop; re-triage at next deploy'). This is intentional deferral gated on a deploy, not tier-4 polish displacing tier-1 work. Leave at P4; re-triage at next deploy as the bead itself states.
